repositories.brief_summary: Standard library additions, runtime optimizations, compiler bug fixes, and architecture-specific improvements merged
repositories.new_features_summary: New Lazy.Mutexed module for concurrent code, runtime prefetching optimizations, and multiple architecture bug fixes
repositories.new_features:
- **Lazy.Mutexed module** provides simple mutex-protected lazy thunks for safe concurrent initialization with comprehensive API including `from_fun`, `from_val`, `force`, and `is_val` functions ([#14393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14393))
- **Runtime prefetching improvements** distinguish between read and write access patterns with new `caml_prefetchr` and `caml_prefetchw` primitives, offering performance benefits for multi-domain programs especially on AMD64 with `-mprfchw` ([#14570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14570))
- **s390x heap corruption fix** resolves segfaults in `libasmrun_shared.so` by replacing PLT calls with GOT-indirect calls to runtime glue functions, preventing PLT lazy binding trampoline from corrupting OCaml's fiber stack ([#13693](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13693), [#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547))
- **Obj.dup closure handling** now correctly handles closures and infix tags in `caml_obj_with_tag` runtime function ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492))
- **ARM64 immediate addition fix** corrects code generation for additions with large immediates that was producing wrong assembly with frame pointer support ([#14589](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14589))
- **Linear scan spilling bug fix** resolves miscompilation issue in register allocator's spilling heuristic ([#14583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14583))
- **Documentation improvements** with @since annotations added for new 5.5 submodules and functions ([#14581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14581))
repositories.activity_summary: Extensive testing and CI improvements including TSan output filtering, bootstrap fixes, and RISC-V optimizations
repositories.activity:
- **Standard library enhancements** completed with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) merging the Lazy.Mutexed implementation with review contributions from [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), and the community ([#14393](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14393))
- **Runtime optimizations** delivered by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) with prefetching improvements reviewed by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) and Damien Doligez ([#14570](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14570)), plus runtime cleanup work ([#14569](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14569))
- **Architecture-specific bug fixes** including [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham)'s s390x heap corruption resolution reviewed by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) ([#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547)), and [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy)'s ARM64 immediate addition fix ([#14589](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14589), [#14606](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14606))
- **Compiler bug fixes** with [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixing linear scan register allocation ([#14583](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14583)) and [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) correcting Obj.dup behavior ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14492))
- **Test infrastructure improvements** by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) with TSan output filtering for portability ([#14588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14588)) and multiple Lazy.Mutexed testsuite fixes ([#14591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14591), [#14597](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14597))
- **CI maintenance** completed by [Sébastien Hinderer](https://github.com/shindere) fixing Inria's bootstrap job ([#14595](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14595)) and code formatting ([#14592](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14592)), plus [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) optimizing RISC-V testing to save 45 minutes per run ([#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14579))
- **API consistency work** with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reverting Iarray API changes ([#14584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14584)), [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) adding missing @since annotations ([#14581](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14581)), and [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) adding type system test coverage ([#14582](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14582))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on multi-domain runtime safety, architecture-specific correctness, and test infrastructure robustness
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Multi-domain safety** initiatives advancing with new synchronization primitives like Lazy.Mutexed and runtime optimizations specifically targeting concurrent workloads across multiple domains
- **Architecture-specific correctness** receiving attention with fixes for s390x, ARM64, and RISC-V platforms demonstrating commitment to broad platform support and correctness
- **Test infrastructure robustness** improving through systematic work on TSan output portability, CI optimization, and comprehensive test coverage for new features
- **Runtime performance focus** evident in prefetching improvements and register allocation optimizations targeting real-world performance bottlenecks

repositories.brief_summary: Updated Merlin integration to handle new enclosing parameter and switched CI to use upstream Merlin repository
repositories.new_features_summary: Updated selection range API to support new optional parameter in Merlin enclosing queries
repositories.new_features:
- Updated **selection range** functionality to handle new optional parameter in Merlin's `Enclosing` query, passing `None` as the second parameter to maintain compatibility with upstream [ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029) changes ([#1590](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1590))
- Switched CI workflow to use **upstream Merlin repository** (`ocaml/merlin`) instead of fork (`voodoos/merlin`) for development builds, aligning with upstream integration ([#1590](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1590))
repositories.activity_summary: One PR opened for automated flake.lock updates, one merged for Merlin integration, and one issue reported about Reason documentation
repositories.activity:

- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) merged PR updating **Merlin integration** to handle new enclosing parameter, modifying the `Enclosing` query call to pass an optional second parameter as `None` ([#1590](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1590))
- [sirlan](https://github.com/sirlan-ff00ff) reported **Reason documentation issue** where LSP doesn't display doc comments for Reason code in hover or autocomplete, only showing signatures, related to upstream [reasonml/reason#2815](https://github.com/reasonml/reason/issues/2815) ([#1589](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1589))

- **Reason language support** gaps identified, particularly around documentation comment handling, highlighting areas where the LSP may need enhancements for alternative OCaml syntaxes ([#1589](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1589))

repositories.brief_summary: LSP improvements with new enclosing range parameter and signature help fixes, plus Windows MSVC build compatibility
repositories.new_features_summary: New `-end-position` parameter for enclosing command and signature help now works within `let..in` bindings
repositories.new_features:
- Added **`-end-position` parameter** to the `enclosing` command, allowing queries for entities containing a specific range rather than just a point position, enabling better support for region-based editor features like type annotation of selected expressions ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- Fixed **signature help** to appear correctly within `let..in` bindings even when the `in` keyword hasn't been written yet, improving the editing experience when writing function applications inside let-bound expressions ([#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- Fixed **MSVC build compatibility** with OCaml 5.4 by reordering Windows header includes to resolve Winsock1/Winsock2 conflicts ([#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038))
- Updated **protocol documentation** to reflect the new `-end-position` parameter for the `enclosing` command ([fad4a27](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/fad4a2785c6d860b392b977eb79f73040174eb30))
repositories.activity_summary: PATH_MAX portability discussion ongoing, three PRs merged for LSP improvements and Windows compatibility
repositories.activity:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) **opened PR** to define `PATH_MAX` to 4096 when undefined for better portability across systems without fixed path limits ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039)), with discussion about best practices referencing GNU Hurd documentation and suggesting the value be increased to 8192 based on gnulib precedent
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) **merged improvements** to the `enclosing` command by adding range support through the new `-end-position` parameter, enabling LSP extensions like `locate-types` to handle expression fragments rather than just point positions ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) **fixed signature help behavior** for `let..in` bindings by implementing fallback logic that searches for arrow-typed expressions in let-bound values when the cursor is positioned before the `in` keyword ([#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) **resolved MSVC build issues** with OCaml 5.4 by moving `windows.h` include after Caml headers to avoid Winsock version conflicts ([#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) **published release** v5.7-505_preview with fixes for locate-types, record field autocompletion, and signature help parameter handling from previous weeks' work
repositories.discussions_summary: Technical discussion about PATH_MAX portability and proper handling of path length limits
repositories.notable_discussions: - Discussion on [#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039) about **`PATH_MAX` portability** with [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) referencing GNU Hurd documentation explaining that well-designed code should avoid fixed path limits entirely, with links to POSIX 2024 standards and historical blog posts about the limitations of `PATH_MAX` assumptions, ultimately recommending doubling the value to 8192 as an interim solution
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on LSP protocol enhancements and editor integration improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Range-based LSP queries** are being expanded beyond point-based positions, with the new `-end-position` parameter for `enclosing` enabling features like type annotation of selected code regions ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Signature help robustness** improvements continue with fixes for edge cases in `let..in` expressions, building on recent work addressing labeled and optional parameters ([#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036), [#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032))
- **Windows platform support** receiving attention with MSVC-specific build fixes for OCaml 5.4 compatibility ([#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038))
- **Portability discussions** around system-dependent constants like `PATH_MAX` showing awareness of cross-platform compatibility concerns ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039))
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors merged code improvements, with additional review and release work
repositories.contributors:
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) implemented the `-end-position` parameter for the `enclosing` command ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029)), including protocol changes, kernel updates with new `range_enclosing` function in `mbrowse.ml`, and comprehensive test cases
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) fixed signature help behavior in `let..in` bindings ([#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036)) by adding fallback logic to search for arrow-typed expressions when cursor is before the `in` keyword, with extensive test coverage
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) resolved MSVC build compatibility issues with OCaml 5.4 ([#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038)) by fixing header include ordering
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened PR for `PATH_MAX` portability improvements ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed and merged PRs ([#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029), [#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036), [#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038)) and published the v5.7-505_preview release
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) provided technical review and portability guidance on `PATH_MAX` handling ([#2039](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2039)) and MSVC compatibility ([#2038](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2038))

repositories.brief_summary: Rev store performance improvements, code quality refactoring, and package management optimization planning
repositories.new_features_summary: Rev store tip negotiation, Melange shared cache, OpenBSD tar support, Rocq output tests, diagnostic log fixes, C stubs improvements
repositories.new_features:
- **Rev store tip negotiation** eliminates full git history fetches by storing references of fetched commits for later protocol negotiation, delivering performance improvements for package management workflows ([#13659](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13659), [#13598](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13598), [#13672](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13672)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) fixing [#13323](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13323)
- **Melange shared cache** support allows Melange targets to be declared for shared caching, improving build performance for JavaScript/ReScript projects ([#13655](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13655)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **OpenBSD tar support** with explicit compression flags (-z, -j) ensures cross-platform package handling ([#13685](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13685)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Rocq expected output tests** with proper module handling and language version guarding ([#13632](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13632), [#13684](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13684)) by [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Diagnostic log location** updated in error messages to point to `_build/trace.csexp` instead of removed `_build/log` ([#13683](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13683)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixing [#13679](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13679)
- **C stubs Caml header dependencies** properly specified to fix underspecified build dependencies ([#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652)) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixing [#13651](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13651)
repositories.activity_summary: Code quality refactoring of diff actions, package management improvements, release documentation, and package management optimization planning
repositories.activity:
- **Diff action refactoring** by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) removing unnecessary optionality ([#13690](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13690)), moving Path.Permissions to Stdune ([#13693](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13693)), improving path handling ([#13694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13694)), simplifying fallback logic ([#13695](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13695)), and enhancing test coverage ([#13689](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13689)), plus [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) applying diff actions to CI script promotion ([#13662](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13662))
- **Package management improvements** including removing unnecessary vendor cleanup step ([#13664](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13664)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) and install circular dependency fix ([#13692](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13692)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) resolving [#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771)
- **Release documentation** moved into repository at `doc/dev/releases/process.md` ([#13681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13681)) with updated checklist template ([#13673](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13673)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), plus 3.22.0 release tracker opened ([#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674))
- **Testing improvements** including ctypes module exclusion bug test ([#13700](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13700)), removal of --display short flag ([#13661](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13661)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), and Nix infrastructure updates ([#13701](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13701), [#13702](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13702), [#13703](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13703)) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **Package management optimization planning** by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) with systematic issues exploring lock time simplification: converting undefined variables to defaults ([#13660](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13660)), simplifying `%{pkg-self:name}` ([#13665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13665)), `%{pkg:foo:dev}` variables ([#13666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13666)), `%{pkg-self:pinned}` substitution ([#13667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13667)), depext filtering with platform values ([#13668](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13668)), boolean variable reduction ([#13669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13669)), `%{pkg:other-pkg:installed}` substitution ([#13670](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13670)), rev_store relevant tips negotiation ([#13671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13671)), depexts compiler build issue ([#13677](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13677)), plus tar unpacking/decompression separation ([#13687](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13687)) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV)
- **Documentation issues** for depexts ([#13657](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13657)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) and variable example values ([#13675](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13675)) by [Andre](https://github.com/maroneze)
- **Other issues** including RPC binary lookup ([#13676](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13676)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), Windows --release flag behavior ([#13658](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13658)) by [Andre](https://github.com/maroneze), relocatable ocamlbuild ([#13678](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13678)), and portable lock file format behavior changes ([#13680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13680)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **Issues closed** including C stubs underspecification ([#13651](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13651)), full git history fetching ([#13323](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13323)), 3.21.1 release completion ([#13557](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13557)), diagnostic log message ([#13679](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13679)), plus old issues on overcaching ([#4604](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4604)) and dune file syntax ([#2355](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/2355))
repositories.trends_summary: Package management maturation through lock file optimization, git fetching performance, cross-platform robustness, and developer experience improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Package management maturation** with [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) systematically identifying lock file optimization opportunities through variable substitution and filtering at lock time rather than build time ([#13660](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13660), [#13665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13665), [#13666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13666), [#13667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13667), [#13668](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13668), [#13669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13669), [#13670](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13670), [#13671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13671))
- **Git fetching performance** improvements through rev_store tip negotiation eliminating full history fetches, particularly beneficial for CI/CD workflows ([#13659](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13659), [#13323](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13323))
- **Cross-platform robustness** focus with OpenBSD tar support ([#13685](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13685)) and Windows compatibility discussions ([#13658](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13658))
- **Alternative build systems support** expanding Dune's ecosystem beyond OCaml with Melange shared cache ([#13655](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13655)) and Rocq expected output tests ([#13632](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13632), [#13684](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13684))
- **Developer experience improvements** through better error messages ([#13683](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13683)), release documentation ([#13681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13681)), and code quality refactoring ([#13690](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13690), [#13693](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13693), [#13694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13694), [#13695](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13695))
repositories.contributors_summary: Six contributors merged 20 PRs with code refactoring, package management improvements, release documentation, and optimization planning
repositories.contributors:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) refactored the diff action system removing unnecessary optionality ([#13690](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13690)), moving Path.Permissions to Stdune ([#13693](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13693)), improving path handling ([#13694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13694)), simplifying fallback logic ([#13695](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13695)), enhancing tests ([#13689](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13689)), and removing --display short flag ([#13661](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13661))
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) implemented rev store tip negotiation ([#13659](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13659), [#13598](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13598), [#13672](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13672)) fixing [#13323](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13323), added OpenBSD tar support ([#13685](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13685)), removed vendor cleanup step ([#13664](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13664)), applied diff actions to CI ([#13662](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13662)), guarded Rocq tests ([#13684](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13684)), and opened 15 issues for package management optimization including lock time simplification ([#13660](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13660), [#13665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13665), [#13666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13666), [#13667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13667), [#13668](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13668), [#13669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13669), [#13670](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13670), [#13671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13671), [#13677](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13677)), depexts documentation ([#13657](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13657)), and RPC binary lookup ([#13676](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13676))
- [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) added Melange shared cache support ([#13655](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13655)), fixed install circular dependencies ([#13692](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13692)) resolving [#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771), added ctypes crash test ([#13700](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13700)), and updated Nix infrastructure ([#13701](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13701), [#13702](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13702), [#13703](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13703))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixed diagnostic log location messages ([#13683](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13683)) resolving [#13679](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13679), moved release documentation into repository ([#13681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13681)), updated release checklist ([#13673](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13673)), and opened issues for 3.22.0 release tracker ([#13674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13674)), relocatable ocamlbuild ([#13678](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13678)), and lock file format behavior ([#13680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13680))
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixed C stubs Caml header dependencies ([#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652)) resolving [#13651](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13651)
- [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) added Rocq expected output tests ([#13632](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13632))
- [Andre](https://github.com/maroneze) opened documentation issues for variable example values ([#13675](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13675)) and Windows --release flag behavior ([#13658](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13658))
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) opened issue for tar unpacking/decompression separation ([#13687](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13687))

repositories.brief_summary: Documentation overhaul week with new tutorials, navigation improvements, and infrastructure refactoring
repositories.new_features_summary: New tutorials for GitHub Actions, Docker, and OCaml-CI; improved opam switches guide; governance calendar with iCal export
repositories.new_features:
- New **GitHub Actions tutorial** guides users through setting up OCaml in CI workflows with practical examples ([#3458](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3458))
- New **Docker tutorial** explains using official OCaml Docker images for containerized development ([#3457](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3457))
- New **OCaml-CI tutorial** documents using OCaml's continuous integration service for projects ([#3456](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3456))
- Completely **rewritten opam switches tutorial** with step-by-step walkthrough covering global vs local switches, creation, switching, and removal with updated examples for OCaml 5.4.0 ([#3526](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3526))
- **Governance page** now accessible via Community tab navigation making team information more discoverable ([#3538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3538))
- New **governance dev meetings calendar page** at `/governance/dev-meetings-calendar` with calendar-style view and iCal export capability ([#3536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3536))
- **Infrastructure link** added to Tools mobile dropdown fixing navigation discoverability on phones ([#3542](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3542))
- New **opam-repository infrastructure page** added to help users discover the package repository and related services ([#3407](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3407))
- Enhanced **package documentation page** with new section explaining automatic odoc generation via ocaml-docs-ci and improved missing docs error page with better reporting flow ([#3471](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3471))
- Updated **Emacs editor setup tutorial** with clearer guidance on major modes (Tuareg, Caml, Neocaml) and LSP configuration including tree-sitter details ([#3509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3509))
- **FUN OCaml 2024 and 2025** conference data now includes direct links to individual talk detail pages on fun-ocaml.com ([#3539](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3539))
- **Talk detail links** added for 25 presentations across FUN OCaml 2024 (14 talks) and 2025 (11 talks) ([#3539](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3539))
repositories.activity_summary: Build system refactoring, extensive documentation cleanup, and multiple content fixes across the site
repositories.activity:
- **Build system refactoring** replaced ood-gen code generator with data-packer and data-scrape, reducing CPU usage by 2.5x and output size by 7x while improving build times by 15% ([#3463](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3463))
- **RSS feed repair** removed debug output from data-packer that was breaking planet.xml, news.xml, and other feeds with prepended loader messages ([#3530](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3530))
- **Playground error handling** improved with fail-fast validation when default example is missing, fixing 500 errors on `/play` endpoint ([#3535](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3535))
- Extensive **markdown linting and typo fixes** across 87 exercise files ([#3494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3494)), root-level and developer docs ([#3493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3493)), and 50+ data files ([#3495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3495)) improving consistency and fixing broken links
- **Academic institutions page** updated with fixed course URLs for Ashoka University, Caltech CS 164, BITS Pilani, and archived links for Universidade da Beira Interior ([#3537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3537))
- **Paper metadata correction** fixed wrong abstract for "Optimizing Pattern Matching" paper that was incorrectly copied from garbage collection paper ([#3534](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3534))
- **Platform Newsletter** for November 2025 to January 2026 added with changelog announcements for OCaml 5.4.1, 4.14.3, and Dune 3.21.1 releases ([#3519](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3519), [#3522](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3522), [#3524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3524), [#3525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3525))
- **Release title corrections** added tool names to bare version numbers for opam-publish 2.7.1, opam 2.5.0, and Merlin releases ([#3485](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3485), [#3525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3525))
- **Dependency cleanup** removed pin on aantron/river fork, now using stock river 0.4 from opam-repository ([#3515](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3515))
- **Markdown linter configuration** updated to disable MD060 table-column-style rule and add siblings_only to MD024 for better compatibility ([#3523](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3523))
- **Governance data update** removed inactive OCaml.org dev meeting entry from governance.yml ([#3540](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3540))
- **Code formatting** applied ocamlformat to code example parser ([#3541](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3541))
- **Ignored platform releases** functionality restored to properly filter out releases marked as ignored ([#3482](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3482))
- **Hash table documentation** typo fix removing stray 'p' character ([#3529](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3529))
- **GitHub Actions tutorial fix** corrected OCaml compiler version format requiring quotes for 4.14 to prevent YAML float parsing ([#3545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3545))
- **Automated blog scraping** merged new OCaml Planet posts from bbatsov (Emacs TreeSitter and Neocaml), anil (Docker CACM and AI summit), jonludlam (weeknotes), and cwn (weekly news) ([#3521](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3521), [#3532](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3532), [#3543](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3543))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on opam 3.0 release announcement scope and governance calendar implementation approach
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Discussion about **opam 3.0 release announcement** scope with [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) explaining that while version was bumped due to breaking changes (option prefixes disallowed, one option removed), the release consists mostly of fixes and niche features affecting only a small number of users ([#3498](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3498))
- Conversation about **governance dev meetings calendar** feature request led to proposal for dedicated subpage rendering governance events alongside community events, with implementation now in progress ([#3305](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3305))
- Review feedback on **Emacs tutorial updates** suggesting removal of marketing-style language in favor of clearer feature descriptions for Neocaml mode ([#3509](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3509))
- Suggestion to add **CLAUDE.md** to project with markdown linter instructions to help AI-assisted contributions respect project standards ([#3526](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3526))
repositories.trends_summary: Infrastructure modernization, documentation quality improvements, and enhanced discoverability of OCaml ecosystem tools
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Infrastructure tutorial expansion** continuing with three new tutorials (GitHub Actions, Docker, OCaml-CI) making OCaml's development infrastructure more accessible to newcomers and demonstrating ongoing commitment to onboarding documentation ([#3456](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3456), [#3457](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3457), [#3458](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3458))
- **Build system optimization** focus with data-packer refactoring achieving 2.5x CPU reduction and 7x output size reduction, showing attention to developer experience and CI performance ([#3463](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3463))
- **Navigation and discoverability improvements** across the site with governance page exposure, mobile dropdown fixes, and better linking between related pages indicating user experience refinement ([#3162](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3162), [#3466](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3466), [#3538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3538), [#3542](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3542))
- **Content quality and accuracy** emphasis with systematic markdown linting, typo fixes, and metadata corrections across 100+ files showing maturation of content management ([#3493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3493), [#3494](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3494), [#3495](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3495))
- **Tutorial rewrites for clarity** with opam switches getting complete overhaul and managing dependencies improvements, prioritizing newcomer comprehension over terseness ([#3526](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3526))
- **Conference content enrichment** adding direct talk links and improving conference data completeness to better serve the OCaml community's event resources ([#3539](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3539))

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 released with compiler cloning support; 40+ packages merged including mosaic terminal UI framework and raven ML updates
repositories.new_features_summary: Compiler cloning for relocatable switches, mosaic TUI framework with flexbox/grid layout, OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 release
repositories.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1** compiler release by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) with new **compiler-cloning** meta-package enabling relocatable compiler switches that can be duplicated without recompilation when using the same compiler version and configuration ([#29462](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29462))
- **Mosaic 0.1.0** terminal UI framework by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) with React-inspired hooks, **Elm Architecture** (Model-View-Update), **Flexbox and CSS Grid layout** via Toffee engine, rich component library including Box/Text/Canvas/Code with syntax highlighting, designed for **60+ FPS** performance with minimal allocations ([#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **Matrix 0.1.0** low-level terminal library (part of mosaic) with immediate-mode runtime, full Unicode support, modern terminal protocols (Kitty keyboard, SGR mouse tracking, bracketed paste), native alpha blending, PTY/VTE support, and automatic capability detection ([#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **Toffee 0.1.0** CSS layout engine ported from Rust Taffy implementing CSS Grid Level 1, Flexbox, and Block layout algorithms in pure OCaml with zero dependencies ([#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **Mirage 4.10.5** with **DHCP enabled by default** and charrua 3.0 update, improving network configuration out-of-the-box for unikernels ([#29454](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29454))
- **DNS 10.2.4** with improved **resolver error propagation** through new `Dns_client.request_error` variant, providing better visibility into DNS resolution failures ([#29455](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29455))
- **Lwt 6.1.1** with new **`Lwt_stream.create_and_push`** function for creating streams with an explicit push function, enabling imperative stream construction ([#29449](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29449))
- **Sqlite3 5.4.0** adds **`column_is_null`** function to check if a column value is NULL without converting to OCaml option type ([#29436](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29436))
- **ambient-context 0.2** with **breaking API changes** to support both Eio and Lwt backends for thread-local storage, requiring new `ambient-context-eio` and `ambient-context-lwt` sub-libraries ([#29418](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29418))
- **qcow 0.13.0** with **breaking changes** including memory optimizations, improved error handling, and refactored block device interface ([#29420](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29420))
- **kqueue 0.7.0** adds **`EVFILT_USER` and `EVFILT_SIGNAL`** event filters, simpler event addition interface avoiding record/callback allocation, and **fold for events** operation ([#29489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29489))
- **conf-zlib 1** with extensive platform updates adding support for **centos, suse, opensuse, freebsd, msys2** systems and improved fedora/debian compatibility ([#29394](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29394))
- **conf-bzip2 1** with **MinGW support** via new `conf-mingw-w64-bzip2` packages and cygwin fixes for systems without pkgconf ([#29395](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29395))
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing PRs include raven alpha3 ML framework rewrite, elpi 3.6, ortools updates, ciao_lwt migration tool, and syndic RSS parser
repositories.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release tracking** with new issue [#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463) opened by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) to track platform tools and ecosystem readiness for OCaml 5.5, replacing closed 5.4.0 tracking issue ([#27916](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/27916))
- **Raven 1.0.0~alpha3** draft PR [#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) includes major ML framework rewrite with new **nx-oxcaml** pure-OCaml tensor backend using SIMD approaching C backend performance, **kaun-board** TUI training monitor, **brot** tokenization library (renamed from saga) claiming 1.3-6x speedup over HuggingFace, unified Nx/Rune tensor types, and complete quill notebook rewrite with terminal UI and web frontend
- **TextMate grammars** ecosystem emerging with **tm-grammars 1.0.0** ([#29474](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29474)) by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) bundling multiple language grammars (initially proposed as 40+ separate packages, consolidated to single package with sub-libraries following [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggestion) and **textmate-language 0.5.0** ([#29488](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29488)) by [Alan](https://github.com/alan-j-hu) fixing OCaml grammar compatibility and handling captures as lists
- **Syndic 1.8.0** RSS/Atom parser PR [#29478](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29478) by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) fixing timezone handling for RFC822 dates, adding documentation contributions, and allowing selectively relaxed parsing rules, awaiting OCaml version constraint fix
- **elpi 3.5.0** PR [#29470](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29470) by [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares) closed after discussion of OCaml 4.14 arm64 assembler errors; maintainer will release 3.6 using menhir `--table` mode to reduce code size and avoid OCaml compiler bug affecting 4.14.2-4.14.3, following suggestion by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy)
- **ortools 9.15.0-1** update PR [#29483](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29483) by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk) removes eigen3 depext and adjusts conf-zlib/conf-bzip2 dependencies after recent work on those packages; Linux and macOS builds working but MSYS2 fails on zlib detection
- **ciao_lwt 0.1** migration tool PR [#29477](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29477) by [Jules Aguillon](https://github.com/Julow) for converting Lwt code to direct-style concurrency requires opam constraint fixes for cmdliner and ocaml version bounds
- **trace 0.12** observability library PR [#29471](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29471) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) adds ambient span provider concept, thread-local-storage library, runtime events collector, and thread/process sorting extensions, needs OCaml >= 5.1 constraint for Runtime_events.Type
- **biotk 0.4** bioinformatics toolkit PR [#29486](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29486) by [pveber](https://github.com/pveber) with API changes and bug fixes for Bam, Sam, and Pwm modules
- **colibri2 0.5** CP solver PR [#29484](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29484) by [François Bobot](https://github.com/bobot) adds propagators for floating-point and bitvector theories, arrays/sequences support, linear boolean ring for BV, labelled union-find for LRA, and propagators for abs/power/modulo operations

repositories.discussions_summary: MetaOCaml installation issue with ocaml-option-tsan and macOS switch creation failure redirected to opam repo
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **MetaOCaml incorrectly installed** with `opam switch create ocaml-option-tsan` command due to solver choosing `ocaml-variants.x.y+BER` with metaocaml as post-dependency instead of `ocaml-variants.x.y+options`, discussed in issue [#28249](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28249) opened by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) with workaround suggestion to use explicit `ocaml-variants.<version>+options` in switch creation; [Alina Banerjee](https://github.com/alinab) and [KC Sivaramakrishnan](https://github.com/kayceesrk) provided working command, potential fix involves adding `avoid-version` flags to metaocaml packages
- **macOS switch cloning failure** reported in issue [#29479](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29479) where second switch creation for OCaml 5.5.0~alpha fails with "cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted" error at line 23 of clone script, affecting heredoc operations; issue closed and redirected to [opam repo issue #6861](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6861) by [@jrfondren](https://github.com/jrfondren)
- **Immutable policy discussion** continues in issue [#29072](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29072) regarding stricter enforcement of package immutability, pin-depends usage, and reproducibility concerns raised by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm)
- **Package naming and renaming** discussion in raven PR [#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) where [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) asks about defined process for renaming packages (saga→brot in this release), considering removal of previous raven packages from opam-repository given limited user base and stabilizing APIs
- **TextMate grammars package design** discussion in PR [#29474](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29474) where [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggested consolidating 40+ individual `tm-grammar-<language>` packages into single `tm-grammars` package with findlib submodules to reduce opam solver pressure while maintaining simple dependency cone; [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) agreed and updated PR accordingly
- **elpi assembler error** in PR [#29470](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29470) discussed by [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares), [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust), [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy), and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) where OCaml 4.14.2-4.14.3 arm64 compiler generates "conditional branch out of range" errors for large grammar.ml file; [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy) suggested using menhir `--table` mode to reduce code size and confirmed fix will be in OCaml 4.14.4
repositories.trends_summary: Terminal UI frameworks emerging, OCaml 5.x maturity with effects-based libraries, Windows/cross-platform support improvements, package relocatability focus
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Terminal UI renaissance** with multiple new frameworks including mosaic (React-inspired hooks, Elm Architecture, Flexbox/Grid), kaun-board (ML training monitoring), vif (OCaml 5 effects-based web framework), and windtrap (unified testing) demonstrating growing interest in rich terminal applications
- **OCaml 5.x ecosystem maturity** shown through 5.5.0~alpha1 release with compiler cloning feature, effects-based libraries (vif, ciao_lwt migration tool, ambient-context 0.2 with Eio support), and runtime events integration (trace 0.12), indicating transition from experimental to production-ready effects usage
- **Machine learning investment** visible in raven ecosystem evolution with pure-OCaml tensor backend (nx-oxcaml) using SIMD to approach C performance, specialized TUI tooling (kaun-board), high-performance tokenization (brot), demonstrating commitment to competitive ML tooling in OCaml
- **Cross-platform and Windows support** emphasis through extensive conf-package updates (conf-zlib adding centos/suse/opensuse/freebsd/msys2, conf-bzip2 MinGW support, conf-oniguruma cygwin fixes) and Windows-specific build system improvements, reducing historical Windows support gap
- **Package relocatability initiative** via new `relocatable` virtual package ([#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451)) and compiler-cloning feature preparing ecosystem for Dune package management and improved switch portability
- **Syntax highlighting ecosystem** emerging with TextMate grammar support (tm-grammars, textmate-language), tree-sitter integration, and Code component in mosaic, enabling consistent syntax highlighting across terminal applications
- **MirageOS unikernel development** continues with DNS improvements (10.2.4 error propagation), mirage 4.10.5 (DHCP by default), new mhttp library, qcow 0.13.0 memory optimizations, and ocaml-solo5 updates maintaining active unikernel ecosystem
- **Web framework diversity** increasing with vif (effects-based), Eliom updates (12.0.1, ocsigen-start 8.0.0), html_of_jsx (JSX syntax), mhttp (unikernel HTTP), showing multiple approaches to web development in OCaml
- **Testing framework consolidation** attempted by windtrap 0.1.0 unifying unit, property-based, snapshot, and expect testing paradigms to address fragmented testing landscape
- **Scientific computing focus** with biotk 0.4 (bioinformatics), colibri2 0.5 (SMT-LIB CP solver with FP/BV/arrays), ortools 9.15.0 (Google OR-Tools bindings), ppx_mikmatch 1.2 (regex matching) supporting computational research workflows

repositories.brief_summary: 15 PRs merged with compiler backend optimizations, type system improvements, and extensive test coverage expansion.
repositories.new_features_summary: Scannable pointerness axis, backend register refactoring, x86_64 lea optimizations, and runtime prefetch improvements.
repositories.new_features:
- **Scannable pointerness axis** ([#5484](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5484)) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) added support for `non_pointer` annotations in type declarations, enabling more precise control over garbage collector scanning behavior with comprehensive parsing, type checking, and test coverage.
- **Backend register architecture refactoring** ([#5308](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5308)) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) redesigned physical register handling in AMD64 and ARM64 backends, replacing magic numbers with proper variant types and making the `Regs` module the authoritative source for register information, improving type safety and maintainability across 46 files.
- **x86_64 lea instruction optimization** ([#5475](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5475)) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) enhanced code generation to use `lea` for multiplications by 3, 5, and 9, and to reduce register moves, improving performance on x86_64 targets.
- **Runtime GC prefetching** ([#5476](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5476)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) added prefetch instructions to `pool_sweep` with 4 KiB lookahead, optimizing garbage collection performance.
- **Separability soundness fix** ([#5510](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5510)) by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) fixed a critical type system bug where type definitions with constraints could create non-separable types incorrectly classified as separable, preventing potential segmentation faults.
- **DWARF disabled for toplevel** ([#5486](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5486)) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) disabled DWARF debug info generation in the toplevel REPL to work around binary emitter limitations with assembly directives.
- **Internal kind cleanup** ([#5490](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5490)) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) removed `bits64_internal` and other `_internal` kinds, simplifying the kind system.
- **Jekyll documentation improvements** ([#5515](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5515)) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) removed leading numbers from Jekyll link slugs to facilitate page reorganization and reduce broken links.
- **Runtime metaprogramming test directive** ([#5464](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5464)) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) added `#mark_toplevel` directive for expect-tests, enabling better staging checks in runtime metaprogramming tests.
repositories.activity_summary: Major focus on OCaml 5.4 merge preparation, backend testing, and type system development across 8 merged and 11 open PRs.
repositories.activity:
- **5.4 merge conflict resolution**: Multiple PRs merged to resolve conflicts including [#5477](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5477) (driver by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)), [#5469](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5469) (file formats by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)), [#5463](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5463) (middle/backend by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)), [#5442](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5442) (utils by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)), and [#5491](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5491) (CI workflow by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)), with additional PRs in progress for .mli files, parsing, lambda, and bytecomp.
- **Comprehensive backend test coverage** ([#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5447)) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) added 3,300+ lines of `[%%expect_asm]` tests covering allocation, arrays, float_u, int64_u, intrinsics, loops, register allocation, and variants to catch codegen regressions.
- **Old pacing policy removal** ([#5497](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5497)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) cleaned up obsolete GC pacing code after new policy proved effective.
- **Parser-change nag improvements**: Multiple fixes by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) including [#5507](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5507) and [#5501](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5501) to improve developer workflow when parser changes require ppxlib updates.
- **Magic number bump** ([#5500](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5500)) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) for release 5.2.0minus-32.
- **Layout polymorphism work**: Multiple PRs including canonical value descriptions ([#5498](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5498)) and type checking ([#5513](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5513)) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) advancing the `sort_`/`layout_` polymorphism feature.
- **Runtime metaprogramming**: Beta-reducing `eval` in type expressions ([#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5402)) and quote/splice typing fixes ([#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5323)) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski).
- **Zero-alloc on function parameters** ([#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5493)) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) enabling `zero_alloc` attributes on higher-order function arguments.
- **Mode polymorphism printing** ([#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5488)) by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) implementing comprehensive printing of polymorphic mode variables.
- **Better mode hint formatting** ([#5516](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5516)) by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) improving error message formatting for mode errors.
- **Backend optimizations in review**: Register spill/reload improvements ([#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514)) and codegen tests for intrinsics ([#5489](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5489)) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet).
- **More Bigarray locality support** ([#5505](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5505)) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) adding `@local_opt` annotations to sub, change_layout, and size_in_bytes.
repositories.trends_summary: Strong focus on OCaml 5.4 upstream merge preparation and comprehensive backend test infrastructure development.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.4 merge coordination**: Systematic conflict resolution across all compiler subsystems (driver, parsing, lambda, middle-end, backend, utils, file formats) with dedicated CI workflow ([#5491](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5491)) to track compilation progress during merge.
- **Backend test expansion**: Major push to add comprehensive codegen tests ([#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5447), [#5489](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5489)) using `[%%expect_asm]` framework to prevent regressions and validate optimizations.
- **Type system advancement**: Continued development of layout polymorphism ([#5498](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5498), [#5513](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5513), [#5473](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5473)), runtime metaprogramming ([#5402](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5402), [#5323](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5323)), and mode polymorphism ([#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5488)) features.
- **Performance optimization**: x86_64 codegen improvements ([#5475](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5475)), runtime prefetching ([#5476](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5476)), and register allocation enhancements ([#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514)).
- **Type safety and correctness**: Critical bug fixes like separability soundness ([#5510](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5510)) and architectural improvements like backend register refactoring ([#5308](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5308)).
repositories.issues_summary: Two issues tracked: one critical separability bug resolved, one tuple kind issue remains open.
repositories.good_first_issues: - **Tuple kind/subkinding inconsistency** ([#5399](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5399)) reported by [@thebrucecgit](https://github.com/thebrucecgit): discrepancy where returning a tuple directly fails kind checks, but using a let-binding succeeds. Related to incomplete handling of `with` bounds in kind system, targeted for future "fix the kind system" project.

repositories.brief_summary: Active review and discussion on type recovery fix for function application error handling
repositories.activity_summary: Type recovery improvements in function applications under review with maintainer feedback
repositories.activity:
- Type recovery fix for function applications opened by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) addresses issue where locate queries fail with "Not in environment" errors when function arguments have mismatched types ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
- The **error recovery mechanism** was refined by wrapping `unify_exp` calls in `type_argument` with proper error recording instead of propagating exceptions that would discard the typed function identifier from the tree
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) provided review feedback noting the fix appears sound but requires upstream maintainer review, with discussion about whether this represents a regression in cascading error prevention ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion focused on type recovery placement and potential interaction with upstream error handling work
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Maintainers discussed the subtle question of where to perform type recovery in the typing pipeline, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noting that ongoing upstream work by Xavier and Thomas aims to make the recovery process clearer and more maintainable ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
- Concern raised about potential regression in cascading error prevention, suggesting the need for careful evaluation of the error recovery trade-offs ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors engaged in code review and technical discussion on type recovery implementation
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored the type recovery fix for function application error handling with comprehensive test coverage ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) provided initial review feedback on the fix and coordinated upstream maintainer involvement ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) performed detailed technical review, analyzing the fix in context of ongoing upstream error recovery improvements and identifying potential cascading error concerns ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/pull/222))

repositories.brief_summary: Two new PRs opened addressing API improvements and documentation, with ongoing discussions about error handling and temporary directory utilities
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs opened for API improvements and active discussions on error handling and temporary directory utilities
repositories.activity:
- Two **new PRs opened** by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) including `missing_ok` parameter for `Eio.Path.unlink` ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and simplified type syntax in custom env example ([#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832))
- **API enhancement proposal** from [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) to add temporary directory utilities including `Stdenv.tmp` capability and `Path.open_temp_dir`/`Path.with_temp_dir` functions for managing temporary files and directories ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
- **Error handling discussion** initiated by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) regarding missing error handling in `Eio.Net.run_server` when accept fails, proposing default `max_connections=256` and retry mechanism to prevent denial of service scenarios ([#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
repositories.discussions_summary: Proposals for temporary directory utilities and improved error handling in server accept operations
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed comprehensive **temporary directory management** API including `Stdenv.tmp` capability and automatic cleanup functions, seeking feedback on design decisions such as whether to use a separate resource type for security isolation ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
- Discussion on **server resilience** covering proper error handling when `Eio.Net.run_server` accept fails due to file descriptor limits, with proposal for conservative default `max_connections=256` and retry mechanism with configurable wait time ([#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) requested feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on both proposals, emphasizing that even non-attack scenarios need safe defaults to prevent crashes when reaching system file descriptor limits

- **Developer experience focus** evident in proposals for automatic cleanup mechanisms and safer defaults that prevent common programming errors without requiring deep system knowledge

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1 with compiler cloning released; major documentation and infrastructure improvements on ocaml.org
group_summaries.new_features_summary: OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1, mosaic TUI framework, new infrastructure tutorials for GitHub Actions/Docker/OCaml-CI
group_summaries.new_features:
- **OCaml 5.5.0~alpha1** compiler release by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) with new **compiler-cloning** meta-package enabling relocatable compiler switches that can be duplicated without recompilation when using the same compiler version and configuration ([ocaml/opam-repository#29462](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29462))
- **Mosaic 0.1.0** terminal UI framework by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) with React-inspired hooks, **Elm Architecture** (Model-View-Update), **Flexbox and CSS Grid layout** via Toffee engine, rich component library including Box/Text/Canvas/Code with syntax highlighting, designed for **60+ FPS** performance with minimal allocations ([ocaml/opam-repository#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **Matrix 0.1.0** low-level terminal library (part of mosaic) with immediate-mode runtime, full Unicode support, modern terminal protocols (Kitty keyboard, SGR mouse tracking, bracketed paste), native alpha blending, PTY/VTE support, and automatic capability detection ([ocaml/opam-repository#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **Toffee 0.1.0** CSS layout engine ported from Rust Taffy implementing CSS Grid Level 1, Flexbox, and Block layout algorithms in pure OCaml with zero dependencies ([ocaml/opam-repository#29312](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29312))
- **GitHub Actions tutorial** guides users through setting up OCaml in CI workflows with practical examples ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3458](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3458))
- **Docker tutorial** explains using official OCaml Docker images for containerized development ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3457](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3457))
- **OCaml-CI tutorial** documents using OCaml's continuous integration service for projects ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3456](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3456))
- Completely **rewritten opam switches tutorial** with step-by-step walkthrough covering global vs local switches, creation, switching, and removal with updated examples for OCaml 5.4.0 ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3526](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3526))
- **Governance dev meetings calendar page** at `/governance/dev-meetings-calendar` with calendar-style view and iCal export capability ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3536))
- **Mirage 4.10.5** with **DHCP enabled by default** and charrua 3.0 update, improving network configuration out-of-the-box for unikernels ([ocaml/opam-repository#29454](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29454))
- **DNS 10.2.4** with improved **resolver error propagation** through new `Dns_client.request_error` variant, providing better visibility into DNS resolution failures ([ocaml/opam-repository#29455](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29455))
- **Lwt 6.1.1** with new **`Lwt_stream.create_and_push`** function for creating streams with an explicit push function, enabling imperative stream construction ([ocaml/opam-repository#29449](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29449))
- **kqueue 0.7.0** adds **`EVFILT_USER` and `EVFILT_SIGNAL`** event filters, simpler event addition interface avoiding record/callback allocation, and **fold for events** operation ([ocaml/opam-repository#29489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29489))
- **conf-zlib 1** with extensive platform updates adding support for **centos, suse, opensuse, freebsd, msys2** systems and improved fedora/debian compatibility ([ocaml/opam-repository#29394](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29394))
- **conf-bzip2 1** with **MinGW support** via new `conf-mingw-w64-bzip2` packages and cygwin fixes for systems without pkgconf ([ocaml/opam-repository#29395](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29395))
group_summaries.activity_summary: 40+ packages merged including raven ML framework alpha, TextMate grammars, Eliom web framework updates; major build system refactoring on ocaml.org
group_summaries.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release tracking** with new issue [ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463) opened by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) to track platform tools and ecosystem readiness for OCaml 5.5, replacing closed 5.4.0 tracking issue ([ocaml/opam-repository#27916](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/27916))
- **Raven 1.0.0~alpha3** draft PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29490](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29490) by [Thibaut Mattio](https://github.com/tmattio) includes major ML framework rewrite with new **nx-oxcaml** pure-OCaml tensor backend using SIMD approaching C backend performance, **kaun-board** TUI training monitor, **brot** tokenization library (renamed from saga) claiming 1.3-6x speedup over HuggingFace, unified Nx/Rune tensor types, and complete quill notebook rewrite with terminal UI and web frontend
- **TextMate grammars** ecosystem emerging with **tm-grammars 1.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29474](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29474)) by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) bundling multiple language grammars (initially proposed as 40+ separate packages, consolidated to single package with sub-libraries following [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggestion) and **textmate-language 0.5.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29488](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29488)) by [Alan](https://github.com/alan-j-hu) fixing OCaml grammar compatibility and handling captures as lists
- **Syndic 1.8.0** RSS/Atom parser PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29478](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29478) by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) fixing timezone handling for RFC822 dates, adding documentation contributions, and allowing selectively relaxed parsing rules, awaiting OCaml version constraint fix
- **elpi 3.5.0** PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29470](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29470) by [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares) closed after discussion of OCaml 4.14 arm64 assembler errors; maintainer will release 3.6 using menhir `--table` mode to reduce code size and avoid OCaml compiler bug affecting 4.14.2-4.14.3, following suggestion by [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy)
- **ortools 9.15.0-1** update PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29483](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29483) by [Timothy Bourke](https://github.com/tbrk) removes eigen3 depext and adjusts conf-zlib/conf-bzip2 dependencies after recent work on those packages; Linux and macOS builds working but MSYS2 fails on zlib detection
- **Eliom ecosystem updates** with Eliom 12.0.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29481](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29481)), ocsigen-start 8.0.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29467](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29467)), and ocsigen-toolkit 4.2.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29457](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29457)) by [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) advancing the web/mobile application framework
- **Web framework additions** including **vif 0.0.1~beta2** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29458](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29458)) simple web framework for OCaml 5 with effect handlers, **mhttp 0.0.1** HTTP implementation for unikernels, and **html_of_jsx 0.0.8** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29453](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29453)) for rendering HTML with JSX syntax, all by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure)
- **Relocatability initiative** via PR [ocaml/opam-repository#29451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29451) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) adding `relocatable` virtual package to indicate relocatable packages for Dune package management, with conflicts added to all compilers < 5.5, ocamlfind, and ocamlbuild
- **Build system refactoring** replaced ood-gen code generator with data-packer and data-scrape on ocaml.org, reducing CPU usage by 2.5x and output size by 7x while improving build times by 15% ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3463](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3463))
- **RSS feed repair** removed debug output from data-packer that was breaking planet.xml, news.xml, and other feeds with prepended loader messages ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3530](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3530))
